rationale: The cited paper is used in the theoretical setup to support a key premise of the current model: cutting intensity via lower-fat or lower-sugar within-category options is often less enjoyable than the original product. It is also invoked in the contribution framing as prior work that compared substitute type and portion-size reductions while holding frequency constant and focusing on solitary settings. The citing paper then positions its own contribution as adding frequency reduction and social-versus-solitary framing, so the citation plays a meaningful conceptual role rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Liu and Haws (2020) as prior cutback-route research and uses it to define the logic of intensity-based reduction. It extends that line by introducing frequency reduction as a competing route and showing how social versus solitary framing shifts preferences between these cutback options.
